data.title: Ryan Leslie partners with New Wave Press as strategic advisor and investor to revolutionize book publishing.
data.description: A well-known tech entrepreneur and Grammy-nominated music producer, Ryan Leslie, has recently become a strategic advisor and investor at New Wave Press based in New York.

data.title: Solar Alliance Energy Inc. (SOLR: TSX-V, SAENF: OTC) resumes trading on TSXV on Sept 3, 2025 after BCSC cease trade order.
data.description: Solar Alliance Energy Inc., a top solar energy solutions provider specializing in commercial and utility solar sectors, recently shared that its common shares will resume trading on the TSX Venture Exchange ("TSXV") around September 3, 2025. This decision comes after effectively addressing a cease trade order imposed by the British Columbia Securities Commission ("BCSC") on May 7, 2025, which was lifted on May 22, 2025. The company is listed as (TSX-V: SOLR, OTC: SAENF), signifying its presence and impact in the market.

data.title: PharmaResearch partners in Europe, expanding to 22 countries including UK, Germany, France - Aug. 29, 2025.
data.description: PharmaResearch, a prominent regenerative medicine company headed by CEO Jihoon Sohn, has successfully established a strategic distribution network spanning 22 European countries. This network covers crucial markets like the UK, Germany, and France. The development was confirmed on August 29, 2025.

data.title: SF Intra-city achieves record growth in 1H 2025: Revenue up 48.8% to RMB 10.2 billion, net profit reaches RMB 137 million. Delivery order volume increases by 50%. Active merchants grow by 55% to 850,000. 1.14M active riders. Company has RMB 1.1B cash & RMB 1.3B short-term investments.
data.description: Hangzhou SF Intra-city Industrial Co., Ltd. announced its unaudited interim results for the six months ended June 30, 2025, showing impressive growth. Revenue increased by 48.8% YoY to RMB 10,236 million, with a gross profit of RMB 680.8 million. Profit reached approximately RMB 137.0 million, a 120.4% YoY growth. Intra-city delivery service revenue grew by over 50% YoY to RMB 5,778.7 million, with revenue from merchants increasing by 55.4%. The number of active merchants reached 850,000, up 55% YoY, and active consumers surpassed 24.77 million. The company held cash and investments totaling RMB 1,134.1 million and RMB 1,260.4 million respectively. SF Intra-city attributed its success to customer base optimization, lean management, and network scale effects, promising continued investment in growth and efficiency.

data.title: Trump claims to fire Fed Gov. Lisa Cook, she disputes saying he lacks authority. Published by Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: President Donald Trump announced on Monday evening that he is dismissing Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook from her position. Cook responded, stating that Trump does not possess the power to make such a decision. The incident was reported by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Bitcoin miner IREN climbs 14% on $187.3M revenue in latest quarter, driven by BTC, AI growth.
data.description: After reporting a record revenue of $187.3 million in the last quarter, Bitcoin miner IREN saw a 14% increase in after-hours trading. The company is focusing on expanding its operations into AI.

data.title: Celestia token (TIA) trades at $1.71, up 0.35%. Support at $1.59 is vital to avoid a drop towards $1.54.
data.description: Despite bearish technical signals, the stock for TIA is currently trading at $1.71, showing a modest gain of 0.35%. Investors are closely watching the key support level of $1.59, as it plays a crucial role in preventing a deeper correction towards $1.54.

data.title: Bitcoin steady as US GDP grows in Q2; Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin drop. Bitcoin: $111,770.58, Ethereum: $4,453.39, XRP: $2.94, Dogecoin: $0.2200. ETH struggles below $4,500. US GDP on blockchain. $250 million liquidated in crypto, Bitcoin's market dominance down to 57.5%. Top Binance traders reduced long exposure to Bitcoin. Market sentiment neutral.
data.description: Bitcoin remained stable while major altcoins like Ethereum, XRP, and Dogecoin saw losses, despite the U.S. economy showing strong results in Q2. As of 9:20 p.m. ET, Bitcoin was at $111,770.58 (+0.19%), Ethereum at $4,453.39 (-1.26%), XRP at $2.94 (-0.98%), Solana at $213.18 (+4.23%), and Dogecoin at $0.2200 (-0.14%). Ethereum's market dominance grew to 13.9%, surpassing Bitcoin's 57.5%. The U.S. government put GDP figures on the blockchain, aiming to establish the country as the "crypto capital." The market witnessed over $250 million in liquidations in 24 hours, with long liquidations comprising over 60%. Bitcoin's open interest increased 0.30%, although top Binance traders reduced their long exposure to Bitcoin. Market sentiment was "Neutral."
